name: Sanctuary of the Virgen de Herrera by Herrerra de los Navarros — circular from Villar de los Navarros
name_original: Santuario de la Virgen de Herrera por Herrerra de los Navarros — circular desde Villar de los Navarros
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villar De Los Navarros are situated at the foot of the Sierra de Herrera, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features mountainous areas with notable climbs and descents, providing challenging routes. Landscapes include rolling hills and areas with agricultural fields, alongside historical sites. The local environment is characterized by its diverse topography, suitable for experienced touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Villar De Los Navarros The most popular touring cycling route is Sanctuary of the Virgen de Herrera by Herrerra de los Navarros — circular from Villar de los Navarros, a 17.0 miles (27.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and leads to a local sanctuary. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puerto de Badenas loop from Herrera de los Navarros, a difficult 38.5 miles (62.0 km) path. This extensive loop features a mountain pass and offers a demanding cycling experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Badenas – Ascent to Puerto de Rudilla loop from Nogueras, a 21.5 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with an ascent to Puerto de Rudilla, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Villar De Los Navarros is defined by mountainous terrain, rolling hills, and historical Mudejar heritage sites. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists due to the challenging nature of the routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villar De Los Navarros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Villar De Los Navarros?
A: The region around Villar De Los Navarros, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Herrera, offers a land of contrasts. You'll encounter varied terrain, from mountainous areas with significant climbs and descents to rolling hills and agricultural fields. The routes are primarily suited for experienced touring cyclists due to their challenging nature.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: Currently, all touring cycling routes listed around Villar De Los Navarros are rated as difficult. They involve notable elevation gains and challenging distances, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists. There are no easy or moderate routes available in this guide.

Q: What are some of the historical or natural landmarks I can explore while cycling in the area?
A: Villar De Los Navarros is rich in historical and natural sites. You can visit the 15th-century parish church of San Pedro with its unique Mudejar tower. Other notable stops include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Sierra de Herrera&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8027164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monforte de Moyuela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as circular tours. For example, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994072777/sanctuary-of-the-virgen-de-herrera-by-herrerra-de-los-navarros-circular-from-villar-de-los-navarros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Herrera by Herrerra de los Navarros — circular from Villar de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.0 miles (27.3 km) long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Badenas loop from Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;, a more extensive 38.5 miles (62.0 km) path.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route in the Villar De Los Navarros area?
A: The longest touring cycling route featured in this guide is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough – Huesa del Común loop from Monforte de Moyuela&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging route spans 39.5 miles (63.5 km) and typically takes around 4 hours and 2 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, ranging from mountainous areas to semi-desert, suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable cycling conditions. Summers can be hot, especially in more exposed areas, while winters might bring colder temperatures to the higher elevations of the Sierra de Herrera. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant ascents for a challenging ride?
A: Absolutely. The routes around Villar De Los Navarros are known for their challenging ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badenas – Ascent to Puerto de Rudilla loop from Nogueras&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes a notable climb to Puerto de Rudilla. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Badenas loop from Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;, features a mountain pass and an elevation gain of over 750 meters.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in this region?
A: The duration varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994072777/sanctuary-of-the-virgen-de-herrera-by-herrerra-de-los-navarros-circular-from-villar-de-los-navarros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Herrera by Herrerra de los Navarros — circular from Villar de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours and 24 minutes. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough – Huesa del Común loop from Monforte de Moyuela&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours.

Q: What can I expect to see in terms of flora and fauna while cycling?
A: The region's diverse ecosystems, influenced by its proximity to areas like Navarre, offer varied natural beauty. You might cycle through agricultural fields with olive, almond, and orange groves, along riverbanks, and through woodlands. The Sierra de Herrera provides a more rugged environment, potentially offering glimpses of local wildlife and a rich variety of plant life adapted to the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest related to water features along the routes?
A: While not explicitly featuring large waterfalls, the region does have riverine landscapes and points of interest related to water. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough – Huesa del Común loop from Monforte de Moyuela&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough&lt;/a&gt;, a man-made monument that could be an interesting stop.

Q: What is the shortest touring cycling route available in the guide?
A: The shortest touring cycling route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Nogueras&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 14.2 miles (22.9 km) and typically takes around 1 hour and 57 minutes to complete, offering a challenging ride with significant elevation gain.
tips: A beautiful town to get lost in located in the south of the province of Zaragoza, popular with hikers and sports lovers because it is the place chosen to start the ascent to the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera.

The hermitage was erected 500 years ago in the place where, according to tradition, the Virgin appeared to Martín, a charcoal burner from the town who was in the mountains carrying out his work. The paved road ends at the sanctuary itself, so its traffic is limited.
